## 1. Macro / Rates / Inflation

Fed held rates steady at 3.5–3.75% at January 2026 meeting. Market sees low odds (4.4%) of cut at March meeting; expects one cut in 2026. February CPI rose 0.3% m/m, 2.4% y/y — matching expectations. 10Y Treasury yield hit 4.19% on Mar 11 driven by inflation and geopolitical risk.

**Materiality: MEDIUM** — CONFIRMS  
Dovish expectations (one cut in 2026) support negative real yields environment that benefits gold/silver. Sticky inflation near 2.4% reinforces monetary_hedges rationale. Not HIGH because no surprise vs consensus.

## 3. AI / Semiconductor

US Commerce Dept drafted rules requiring government approval for all AI chip exports globally (Bloomberg Mar 5, 2026). BIS revised H200/MI325X license posture from presumption-of-denial to case-by-case review (Jan 15, 2026). H200 exports to China authorized Dec 2025 under strict conditions; ByteDance/Alibaba/Tencent cleared for 400k+ units early 2026.

**Materiality: HIGH** — CONTRADICTS (potential drag on SOXQ/CHAT/DTCR)  
Worldwide export control proposal introduces headline risk for ai_tech sleeve (currently 30%+ target). If enacted, could slow hyperscaler buildouts and semiconductor demand. Not yet final rule (comment period pending), but contradicts bullish AI capex narrative. **Override candidate: None yet** — deferred until rule finalized mid-2026 per source. Gate threshold not breached; momentum still positive across SOXQ/CHAT/GRID.

SEC-CFTC MOU signed Mar 11, 2026 classifying BTC/ETH as commodities; Senate passed CBDC ban 89-10 Mar 12. US spot BTC ETFs logged $458M inflow Mar 2, 2026 (largest Q1 day); BlackRock IBIT led with $263M; total ETF AUM ~$88B.

Recommend: defer override until IBIT crosses zero momentum for 5 consecutive days, given VIX elevated and sector-wide fear (Fear & Greed Index 14).
